methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Watershed, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Taylor Francis, has raised $200M+ to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $1.8B. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparables: 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private firms including KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, Amogy, TAE Technologies, and Redwood Materials.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x multiplier to the $1.8B Series C post-money, implying a next-stage valuation of $3.1B. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables: KoBold Metals at $2.0B, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B, and Amogy at $700M, yielding a median of $1.0B and a range of $700M–$2.0B. The discrepancy between methods reflects heterogeneity in financing outcomes and investor conviction within the climate tech sector.

The triangulated valuation range spans $850M (low) to $3.5B (high), with a median of $2.0B. Watershed's current $1.8B valuation sits within this range but near its lower bound. Key uncertainties include technology maturity, capital efficiency relative to peers, and near-term financing terms. The stage-progression method assumes standard step-up dynamics, while peer comparables are limited to n=3, introducing sensitivity to selection and timing. Market conditions in climate tech remain volatile, making discount and control premiums difficult to anchor.
